cues.label: Evals are the bottleneck
cues.headline: A compliant model, but nothing to trust
cues.narration: The model is coming together, yet nothing ships until it can be evaluated, and today the eval setup is a brittle fifteen page document.

cues.label: Gbench and agentic tasks
cues.headline: A harness that stands up a fake world
cues.narration: Gbench runs academic evals and agentic tasks, seeding fake files and tools, triggering the model as an agent, then inspecting the resulting state.

cues.label: Grade the world, not the words
cues.headline: The action is the truth
cues.narration: Following the Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard and tau-bench, you grade what the model did to a backend, because the sentence can lie and the state cannot.

cues.label: Ditto, Borg, and GCP
cues.headline: The crossover that gates everything
cues.narration: Ditto runs internal-format evals on Borg and pings a model hosted on GCP, and that Borg to GCP hosting link is exactly where Sindhu is stuck.

cues.label: Two serving runtimes
cues.headline: Same weights, different behavior
cues.narration: The model is exercised through vLLM and the Hugging Face implementation, and the eval must hit the runtime you actually intend to certify.

cues.label: One-shot reproducible runs
cues.headline: Infrastructure as code replaces the document
cues.narration: Terraform provisions, Docker pins the code and a benchmark commit, Cloud Run executes one job, results land in durable storage, then everything tears down.

cues.label: The highest-leverage on-ramp
cues.headline: Fix the plumbing, speed up everyone
cues.narration: Making evals reproducible and the hosting solid unblocks every future model change, which is how an infrastructure engineer plugs into model building.
